behavior_summary: This script implements the in-game behavior for a grenade-like throwable item whose visual frame encodes whether it is unlit/unarmed (frame % 4 == 0) or lit/armed (frame % 4 != 0).

U8P_GRENADE::look reports a short description that changes based on the frame modulo (shows 'flask of oil' when unlit, 'lit flask of oil' when armed) and resets an external reference via FREE::resetRef(0x0200, this).

U8P_GRENADE::use arms/ignites the item: it updates the item's animation frame to an armed variant (advancing frames with wrap handling), initializes a local counter (0x06) used in the frame-advance logic, spawns a separate fuse/timer process (FREE::func1C19), then calls a post-change handler METHOD::func0676.

U8P_GRENADE::gotHit handles being struck while unlit: when the first parameter equals zero and the item is unlit (frame % 4 == 0), it throws the item via Item::hurl with randomized speed/velocity, marks the item processed by setting Item::Q, and delegates to a follow-up local routine (U8P_GRENADE::func00FB) to manage subsequent behavior (likely scheduling explosion or state transitions).
U8P_GRENADE::hit handles being struck while lit: if frame % 4 != 0 and Item::Q is not already set, it sets Item::Q and calls METHOD::func0676 to trigger the activation/explosion handler.
notable_control_flow: U8P_GRENADE::look: read Item::getFrame(this) -> FREE::resetRef(0x0200,this) -> if (frame % 4 != 0) bark 'lit flask of oil' else bark 'flask of oil'.
U8P_GRENADE::use: set local1 = 0x06 -> read frames -> compute next-frame or wrapped fallback -> Item::setFrame(...) -> spawn FREE::func1C19(0) (fuse/timer) -> call METHOD::func0676.
U8P_GRENADE::gotHit: if first param == 0 then read frame; if (frame % 4 == 0) call Item::hurl(...) with randomized velocity -> Item::getQ / Item::setQ(1) -> call U8P_GRENADE::func00FB for follow-up.
U8P_GRENADE::hit: read frame; if (frame % 4 != 0) and Item::getQ is false then Item::setQ(1) and call METHOD::func0676.
open_questions: What exact behavior do METHOD::func0676 and U8P_GRENADE::func00FB perform (explosion, damage application, removal, or other)? Their effects are referenced but not present in the excerpt.
What precisely does the item-local field Q represent (a one-time-activation guard, an 'armed' marker, an 'exploded' marker)?
What gameplay meaning is intended for the constant 0x06 used in U8P_GRENADE::use (countdown ticks, fuse length, or just a sentinel)?
Are there additional external or script-level flags elsewhere that interact with this grenade's lifecycle (not shown in this fragment)?

main_function.name: U8P_GRENADE::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short description when the player inspects the grenade and keep an external reference/refcount in sync; distinguishes lit vs unlit state via the item's frame (frame % 4).
entry_conditions: Invoked when the player uses the LOOK/examine action on the item (object 'U8P_GRENADE::look' handler).
Triggered by the engine's U8P_GRENADE::look/inspect process for this item instance (processType 0x0208).

function_map.name: U8P_GRENADE::look
function_map.role: examine/display short description; low-cost maintenance call (FREE::resetRef) to keep references updated

function_map.name: U8P_GRENADE::use
function_map.role: arm/ignite the grenade, update animation frame, spawn fuse/timer, then hand off to a post-U8P_GRENADE::use handler

function_map.name: U8P_GRENADE::gotHit
function_map.role: handle being struck while unlit: convert U8P_GRENADE::hit into a throw/hurl and schedule follow-up behavior

function_map.name: U8P_GRENADE::hit
function_map.role: handle being struck while lit: guard via Item::getQ and trigger post-activation handler

internal_calls.purpose: Follow-up routine invoked after throwing the grenade (likely schedules explosion or continues lifecycle). This routine is referenced but not present in the excerpt.

external_calls.purpose: Read the current animation/frame index to determine whether the item is lit (frame % 4 != 0) or unlit (frame % 4 == 0).
Reset or synchronize an external reference/bookkeeping entry for the item (ref id 0x0200).
Read frame twice to compute base group and next-frame for visual transition when arming.
Update the item's animation/frame to reflect armed/advanced state.
Spawn a separate process that likely implements the fuse/timer or delayed effect after arming.
Post-U8P_GRENADE::use/post-state-change handler invoked after arming (likely triggers common activation logic or further state updates).
Throw/launch the item with type and randomized speed/directions when it is struck while unlit.
Generate random speed component used as an argument to Item::hurl.
Provide randomized velocity components for Item::hurl.
Guard/read to check whether the item has already been processed/activated before setting it.
Mark the item as processed/activated to prevent double-handling after hurling.
Delegate to local follow-up behavior (see internal_calls).
Read frame to determine lit vs unlit and branch behavior accordingly.
Check whether the item was already marked before performing activation actions.
Set processed/activated marker to avoid repeat activations.
Invoke the common activation/explosion handler when a lit grenade is U8P_GRENADE::hit and Q was not set.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script implements the in-game behavior for a grenade-like throwable item whose visual frame encodes whether it is unlit/unarmed (frame % 4 == 0) or lit/armed (frame % 4 != 0).
